title: Quantifying Transcript Abundance from RNA-seq FASTQ Files Using Kallisto
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"brnir","text":"This protocol describes a fast and efficient computational workflow to quantify transcript-level expression from RNA-seq data using the Kallisto tool. Kallisto utilizes pseudoalignment for rapid transcript quantification and is ideal for large datasets. This guide walks through transcriptome index creation, quantification using single- or paired-end FASTQ files, and optional downstream summarization with tximport in R.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"atdpb","text":"Notes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9d8bd","text":"Ensure transcriptome and tx2gene annotations are from the same source (e.g., both Ensembl release 109).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"41kcg","text":"For paired-end data, read pairing must be preserved.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"24etf","text":"Store all output in well-organized directories (one per sample).","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

"},"4":{"type":"code_insert","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":"kallisto quant -i transcriptome.idx -o output_sample1 -b 100 --single -l 200 -s 20 sample1.fastq.gz
"},"5":{"type":"code_insert","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":"library(tximport)
library(readr)

samples <- c("sample1", "sample2")
files <- file.path(samples, "abundance.tsv")names(files) <- samples

tx2gene <- read_csv("tx2gene.csv")
txi <- tximport(files, type = "kallisto", tx2gene = tx2gene)"}}}
steps: Linux/macOS with command-line tools Kallisto installed FASTQ files (single-end or paired-end) Transcriptome reference (FASTA format) Transcript-to-gene mapping file (for downstream summarization using tximport in R)
Step 1: Prepare Transcriptome Index kallisto index -i transcriptome.idx transcripts.fa
Step 2: Quantify Expression from FASTQ For paired-end reads: kallisto quant -i transcriptome.idx -o output_sample1 -b 100 sample1_R1.fastq.gz sample1_R2.fastq.gz
For single-end reads (with estimated fragment length and standard deviation):
Step 3: Summarize at Gene Level with (optional, in R)
From FASTQ to Quant Matrix → FASTQ → Kallisto → abundance.tsv → tximport → count matrix → DESeq2
creator: Sisira Kadambat Nair
